zh.lifeSummary: 當Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda於大約0984年出生在蘇格蘭培爾阿爾時，她的父親King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland27歲，母親Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY23歲。她於1000年與Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl在蘇格蘭培爾阿爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1049年9月15日在蘇格蘭培爾登可爾德享年66歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾易斯爾 本 易納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda于大约0984年出生在苏格兰培爾阿爾时，她的父亲King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland27岁，母亲Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY23岁。她于1000年与Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl在苏格兰培爾阿爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1049年9月15日在苏格兰培爾登可爾德享年66岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰阿吉爾易斯爾 本 易納。

nameMeanings.name: ingen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (western Ashkenazic) and German: habitational name from Bingen in the Rhineland, Germany.
Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead so named with Old Norse bingr ‘cow stall, field, small farm’.

variants.name: Bing
meanings: English (Kent): habitational name from Byng in Suffolk or a topographic name for someone who lived in a hollow (metaphorical use of Middle English bing ‘coffer, bin’). Alternatively, it may be from the Middle English word bing ‘coffer, bin’ (from Old Norse bingr ‘bed, bolster, heap, stook of corn’). This word is the source of the placename.
Jewish (western Ashkenazic) and Danish: habitational name from Bing, a shortened form of   Bingen  .
Danish: metonymic occupational name, from bing ‘storage bin for grain’, for someone who either made or used such containers.

nameMeanings.name: Maíl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Scottish:  see  Meil  .   Black  , at Meal, notes that the surname may not have originated in Shetland but derive from the settlement of some Orkney families there in the early 17th century.
2  Welsh, English:  see  Male  .
3  Irish:  see  Malley  .

variants.name: Malley
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Malley  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in northern France called Mailly (Marne, Somme, and elsewhere).
Americanized form of Rusyn (from Slovakia) Mlej (see Malay ).

variants.name: Male
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Mael (literally ‘prince’).

English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French mail ‘mallet, club, mace’, or Middle English (Old French) maille ‘ring (of chain mail), armour’, or Old French maille ‘speckle, stain’, or Middle English maille (Old French maaille) ‘coin worth half a penny’, or from a shortened form of Anglo-Norman French amel, Old French esma(i)l ‘enamel’ (compare Mailer ). Any of these terms might have been used to characterize an individual's behaviour, occupation, or appearance.

English: nickname Middle English (Old French) male ‘bag, pouch’, also ‘stomach, belly’, perhaps given to someone who wore a distinctive pouch or bag or who made such bags (compare the Middle English occupational term malemakere), or to someone with a prominent belly.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Meil
meanings: Scottish (Orkney): habitational name from one of several places in Orkney (and perhaps Shetland) named from Old Norse melr ‘sandbank’. The surname is now spelled Meil in Orkney and Mail in Shetland. The name is rare in Britain and Ireland.
German: variant of   Meile  .

nameMeanings.name: Coluim
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Irish: from Mac Coluim, see McCallum .

variants.name: McCallum
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Caluim ‘son of Calum or Colum’, a personal name derived from Latin columba ‘dove’. The surname often represents a short form of Mac Gille Caluim or Mac Giolla Coluim ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of Saint Columba’ (Colum Cille). The personal name (Scottish Calum, Irish Colum, originally combined with giolla ‘servant’) has long been popular in Scotland and Ireland, due largely to the cult of Saint Columba, 521–597, who converted the Picts to Christianity and founded an influential monastery on the island of Iona. His name in Irish is Colum Cille ‘Dove of the Church’.

nameMeanings.name: meic
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Bosniak (Mesić and Mešić): patronymic from the Bosniak Muslim personal names Meso and Mešo, pet forms of Mehmed, which is of Turkish and ultimately of Arabic origin (see Muhammad ). It is also found as a Croatian surname, originating from the time of the Turkish occupation of the Balkans.
Croatian (Mesić): nickname for someone who ate a lot of meat, derived from meso ‘meat’.
Slovenian (Mešič) and Croatian (Mešić): nickname from Slovenian mešič, Croatian dialect mešić ‘pouch’. Compare Mesich , Mesick 2, and Messick 2.

variants.name: Muhammad
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Abdullah, Ali, Bilal, Hassan, Ibrahim, Omar, Wali, Ahmad, Yahya, Yusuf, Akbar.

Muslim: from a personal name based on Arabic muḥammad ‘praiseworthy’ (derived from ḥamida ‘praise’; see also Hamid and Mahmud ). This is the name of the founder of Islam, the prophet Muhammad (570–632). Compare Mohammad , Mohammed , and Muhammed .

variants.name: Mesich
meanings: Americanized form of Croatian and Bosniak Mesić and Mešić, Slovenian Mešič (see Mesic ).

en.lifeSummary: When Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda was born about 0984, in Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland, her father, King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland, was 27 and her mother, Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY, was 23. She married Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl in 1000, in Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 15 September 1049, in Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 66, and was buried in Isle of Iona, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.Isle of Iona, Argyll, Escorcia, Scotland: Isle of Iona, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda est née environ 0984, à Atholl, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl en 1000, à Atholl, Perthshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 15 septembre 1049, à Dunkeld, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Isle of Iona, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda um 0984 in Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland 27 und ihre Mutter Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY 23. Sie hat 1000 in Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 15. September 1049 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland und wurde in Isle of Iona, Argyll, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda è nata il circa 0984 nel Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland, sua padre, King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl nel 1000, nel Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 15 settembre 1049, nel Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Isle of Iona, Argyll, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináedaが約0984年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアアソルで生まれた時，父King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotlandは27歳，母Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORYは23歳であった。1000年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアアソルでCrínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Athollと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1049年9月15日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアダンケルドで66歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアルグイルイスル オフ アイオナにて埋葬された。

ko.Isle of Iona, Argyll, Escorcia, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 이슬일 프 이오나
ko.lifeSummary: Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda(이)가 대략 0984년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 앳홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1000년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 앳홀에서 Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1049년 9월 15일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 던켈드에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 이슬일 프 이오나에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda nasceu aproximadamente 0984, em Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland, seu pai, King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl em 1000, em Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 15 de setembro de 1049, em Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Isle of Iona, Argyll, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Isle of Iona, Argyll, Escorcia, Scotland: исл оф иона, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda родилась около 0984 г. в местности атолл, пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl в 1000 г. в местности атолл, пиртшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 15 сентября 1049 г. в местности дункелд, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности исл оф иона, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bethóc ingen Maíl Coluim meic Cináeda nació aproximadamente 0984, en Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland, su papá, King Malcolm MacKenneth II of Scotland, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Aelgifu Sigurdsdottir ou Édith des Orcades d'OSSORY, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Crínán of Dunkeld Mormaer of Atholl en 1000, en Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 15 de septiembre de 1049, en Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Isle of Iona, Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido.
